terms.definition: An adapter that parses external protocol input, calls the shared factory or service seam, and formats protocol output. Connectors must not own business logic.
The centralized business-logic owner. For agent turns, the factory is the unified pipeline in src/engine.
The canonical request-to-response lifecycle that owns validation, session resolution, memory, decomposition, tool execution, inference, guards, persistence, and telemetry for agent turns.
A local user interface such as CLI or TUI that runs in process and may call shared platform service directly instead of using platform REST loopback.
A browser-dashboard websocket topic or control command used for UI snapshots, events, and confirmed controls. It is not an API alias.
An externally addressable HTTP API surface for integration, remote management, bootstrap, debugging, and explicit API-client actions.
Settings that change what the runtime can do without further prompting, including agency, autonomy, scopes, action classes, concurrency, cadence, notification policy, and cloud token budgets.
How proactively FlowForge searches for useful work.
How often FlowForge must ask before executing approved work.
A behavioral release-gate test scenario that strains long-turn, multi-step, and cross-surface agent behavior with quantitative and qualitative scoring.
Structured diagnostic telemetry describing a turn's stages, timings, route, recovery path, and outcome.
A normalized message part for text, file, image, audio, or camera content with provenance, permission, metadata, and retention state.
